findings.message: Architecture table references features/inline-edit and features/settings as distinct modules but neither has a CLAUDE.md sub-file; other comparable modules (core, providers, features/chat, style) all have sub-files
postinstall lifecycle hook runs arbitrary Node.js code on npm install; content is benign (CI guard + file copy) but the hook is an arbitrary-code-execution surface at install time
obsidian dependency uses floating 'latest' tag; future major releases may silently introduce breaking API changes
Most devDependencies use ^ prefix allowing minor and patch upgrades; a compromised minor release in any devDependency would run in the build environment
process.argv args joined as a string and interpolated into execSync template literal; Node execSync(string) invokes sh -c so shell metacharacters enable injection

postinstall lifecycle hook runs arbitrary Node.js code on npm install; content is benign (CI guard + file copy) but the hook is an arbitrary-code-execution surface at install time — scripts.postinstall field present: "node scripts/postinstall.mjs" — code runs automatically on every npm install

process.argv args joined as a string and interpolated into execSync template literal; Node execSync(string) invokes sh -c so shell metacharacters enable injection — execSync(`node esbuild.config.mjs ${args}`, ...) where args = process.argv.slice(2).join(' ') — shell metacharacters in args are interpreted by sh -c

obsidian dependency uses floating 'latest' tag; future major releases may silently introduce breaking API changes — "obsidian": "latest" in devDependencies resolves to npm dist-tag latest at install time
Most devDependencies use ^ prefix allowing minor and patch upgrades; a compromised minor release in any devDependency would run in the build environment

Architecture table references features/inline-edit and features/settings as distinct modules but neither has a CLAUDE.md sub-file; other comparable modules (core, providers, features/chat, style) all have sub-files
